reasoning:
The question asks whether an FSIS recall issued on March 28, 2025, will be marked as "Closed" before July 1, 2025. This allows a window of about 94 days (just over 3 months) for the recall to be completed, verified by FSIS, and successfully updated on the agency's website. The base rate for official closure of federal food recalls (whether FDA or USDA) is typically longer than 3 months, often taking 6-12 months or more. This is because closing a recall requires the company to complete product recovery or destruction, provide documentation, and for the FSIS to conduct and finish its effectiveness checks, followed by an administrative review and website update. 

While the recall involves liquid egg products primarily going to foodservice or institutional customers (which makes tracking and recovery somewhat faster than direct-to-consumer retail), the volume is substantial (over 212,000 lbs). Given standard governmental processing times and the amount of product involved, a ~3-month turnaround to official 'Closed' status is quite aggressive. Given these factors, the base rate suggests it is relatively unlikely to close within this short timeframe.
